sections.paragraph:
ἐνοξίζω (ὀξίζω ‘taste or smell like vinegar’) 1 aor. ἐνώξισα (hapax leg.) to have the taste of fermentation, become sour IMg 10:2.—DELG s.v. ὀξύς.

aor. aor. = aorist

hapax hapax leg. = hapax legomenon (occurring only once)

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 338
